headline: New Ownership of 25-Year-Old PostalAnnex in Tigard, Oregon Brings Fresh Energy to the Business--Serving the Community with Shipping and Office Services
summary: Annex Brands, Inc., a leading franchisor in the packing, shipping, and office services industries, is pleased to announce the successful transfer of ownership of an existing PostalAnnex location in Tigard, OR to new owners, Tom Truong and Nhu-Linh Tran. This transition to new ownership ensures that the community will benefit from the essential shipping and office services provided by this PostalAnnex location for the last 25 years.

content.title: New Ownership of 25-Year-Old PostalAnnex in Tigard, Oregon Brings Fresh Energy to the Business--Serving the Community with Shipping and Office Services
content.summary: Annex Brands, Inc., a leading franchisor in the packing, shipping, and office services industries, is pleased to announce the successful transfer of ownership of an existing PostalAnnex location in Tigard, OR to new owners, Tom Truong and Nhu-Linh Tran. This transition to new ownership ensures that the community will benefit from the essential shipping and office services provided by this PostalAnnex location for the last 25 years.

headline: FedEx, UPS and DHL detail tariff refund approach for customers
summary: The parcel carriers say they will return duties to parties that originally bore the cost for invalidated levies once they receive refunds from Customs and Border Protection.

content.title: FedEx, UPS and DHL detail tariff refund approach for customers

headline: 1 S&P 500 Stock to Research Further and 2 We Find Risky

headline: UPS-owned Happy Returns expands network to 10,000 drop-off locations
summary: Happy Returns, a unit of UPS, is adding 1,700 no-label, no-box returns locations to its network and looking to expand returns service for products that can’t be consolidated. The post UPS-owned Happy Returns expands network to 10,000 drop-off locations appeared first on FreightWaves.

headline: UPS and Happy Returns Hit 10,000-Location Milestone to Dominate Reverse Logistics
summary: The expanded Return Bar network now puts 79% of the U.S. population within five miles of a box-free drop-off point, slashing transit times as annual retail returns top $849 billion.

content.title: UPS and Happy Returns Hit 10,000-Location Milestone to Dominate Reverse Logistics
content.summary: The expanded Return Bar network now puts 79% of the U.S. population within five miles of a box-free drop-off point, slashing transit times as annual retail returns top $849 billion.

headline: UPS and Happy Returns Cement Position as Largest Box-Free, Label-Free Return Network with Expansion to 10,000 U.S. Locations
summary: ATLANTA, April 21, 2026--UPS (NYSE: UPS) and Happy Returns today announced a significant expansion of the Return Bar® network, reaching a new milestone of 10,000 drop-off locations nationwide. This growth adds more than 1,700 locations, primarily through new partnerships with Annex Brands and PackageHub Business Centers®, and removes friction across the e‑commerce experience for retailers and consumers.

content.title: UPS and Happy Returns Cement Position as Largest Box-Free, Label-Free Return Network with Expansion to 10,000 U.S. Locations
content.summary: ATLANTA, April 21, 2026--UPS (NYSE: UPS) and Happy Returns today announced a significant expansion of the Return Bar® network, reaching a new milestone of 10,000 drop-off locations nationwide. This growth adds more than 1,700 locations, primarily through new partnerships with Annex Brands and PackageHub Business Centers®, and removes friction across the e‑commerce experience for retailers and consumers.

headline: UPS, Netflix, BlackRock And A Health Care Stock On CNBC's 'Final Trades'
summary: On CNBC's “Halftime Report Final Trades,” Stephen Weiss, chief investment officer and managing partner of Short Hills Capital Partners, named Netflix, Inc. as his final trade. On the earnings front, Netflix, on Thursday, reported better-than-expected financial results for the first quarter of 2026. The company issued a weak forecast for the second quarter and announced that chairman and co-founder Reed Hastings will not stand for re-election to the board when his term expires in June. Don't Miss
